topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The music swelled, a vibrant wave washing over the ballroom, and I felt a distinct, almost physical tightening in my chest. I spotted him across the crowded floor—tall, impossibly charming, laughing with someone whose hand rested lightly on his arm. My breath hitched. I smoothed down my dress, a nervous flutter starting in my stomach that refused to settle. Every instinct screamed at me to navigate the throng, to find a way across the expanse of polished floor and shared history. I took a deep, bracing breath, forcing a smile onto my face, and started walking toward them, the sound of my heels echoing a frantic rhythm in my ears.

[story 2]

It was chaos, beautiful, loud chaos, and then my eyes locked onto him near the champagne fountain. A jolt, sharp and undeniable, shot right through me, making my fingers clench at my sides. I watched him turn, and for a split second, the entire room seemed to fade into a dull background hum. A sudden, overwhelming urge to bridge the distance between us washed over my composure. I pushed through a group of dancers, my movements suddenly sharper, my focus entirely narrowed onto that single point. When I finally reached him, my voice came out a little breathy, a little too bright.

[story 3]

I was nursing a lukewarm drink, trying to appear casual amidst the glittering throng, when I saw him. He was standing near the entrance, looking entirely too relaxed, and the air around him seemed to shift. A warmth bloomed instantly beneath my ribs, a restless energy that made my feet want to move, to close the gap. I stopped mid-sip, my gaze snagging on him. I straightened my posture, consciously letting my eyes linger for just a moment longer than was strictly necessary. When he finally looked up and caught my stare, a slow, almost imperceptible smile touched my lips, and I knew I couldn't let that moment pass unnoticed.
